overview: From 2026-06-25 to 2026-06-26, elizaos/eliza had 32 new PRs (25 merged), 0 new issues, and 4 active contributors.

topPRs.title: De-larp the benchmark suite: delete unwired benchmarks, wire PARTIAL→REAL, scheduled orchestrator lane + CI-coverage gate (#9475)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
## De-larp the benchmark suite (#9475)

Verified every claim against the code before acting (this is a "verify every benchmark actually benchmarks Eliza" task), then implemented the full checklist. **6 commits**, each test-verified.

### ✅ Delete (LARP / shim / dup — confirmed zero `@elizaos` wiring + zero registry entry)
Deleted the dirs + **fully unwired them from the orchestrator** (they were entangled in `code_agent_matrix.py`, `adapters.py`, `code_agent_coverage.py`, `random_baseline_runner.py`, `runner.py`, `matrix_validation.py`, and ~20 tests — not a simple `git rm`):
- vendored upstreams: `claw-eval`, `qwen-claw-bench`, `swe-bench-pro`, `swe-bench-multilingual`, `loca-bench`
- `qwen-web-bench` (stub), `compactbench` (judges compactors, not the agent), `evm` (abandoned)
- import-shim / dup matrix adapters: `app_eval`, `clawbench_matrix`, `claw_eval_matrix`, `qwen_claw_bench_matrix`, `swe_bench_pro_matrix`, `openclaw_benchmark`
- `skillsbench` / `elizaos_mmau` / `mmau` were already gone on `develop`.
- Also cleaned deletion **stragglers** in KEPT code: removed the orphaned `eliza-adapter` EVM bridge + test, and fixed `app-eval/test_code_agent_coding.py` + docs that imported the removed `benchmarks.app_eval` shim.

### ✅ Wire up (PARTIAL → REAL)
- **rlm-bench**: registry + CLI `--mode` default `stub` → `rlm` (real inference).
- **adhdbench**: `--provider` default → `eliza` (real bridge); `mock-passthrough` now opt-in.
- **experience**: `--mode` default `direct` (no LLM) → `eliza-bridge` (real AgentRuntime).
- **interrupt-bench**: default `scripted` → `cerebras` (real model).
  - *Finding:* the orchestrator adapter layer already routed adhd/experience/interrupt/rlm through the real bridge by default — the larp was only the bare CLI/registry default, now fixed (belt-and-suspenders).
- **three-agent-dialogue**: genuinely IGNORED → **registered** as an orchestrator adapter (command + scorer reading `verification.json` + synthetic calibration payload; satisfies the discovery/calibration/cross-matrix invariants).
- **personality-bench**: already adapter-registered with real runtime (confirmed).
- **app-eval**: its deterministic keyword scorer is **intentional/reproducible** (AGENTS.md). Added an **opt-in** LLM judge (`APP_EVAL_LLM_JUDGE=1`) that *augments* it (default unchanged) — pure, unit-tested blending.

### ✅ Relabel (REAL but not measuring the Eliza agent)
Added honest top-of-README notes to `standard/` (raw-model academic), `scambench` + `abliteration-robustness` (generic provider/model eval), `loadperf/` + `mobile-resource/` (infra/device KPIs).

### ✅ Fix CI + docs (highest value)
- **Scheduled orchestrator lane**: `.github/workflows/benchmark-orchestrator-scheduled.yml` — weekly (+dispatch) real-model run of the core subset (`bfcl,action-calling,agentbench,tau_bench,mint,context_bench`) via `python -m benchmarks.orchestrator run --provider groq`, gated on `GROQ_API_KEY` (skips gracefully), with subset-scoped publishability + artifact upload.
- **Smoke/manual marker + test**: `orchestrator/ci_coverage.py` classifies every registered benchmark into a CI lane (`scheduled`/`smoke`/`manual`); `tests/test_ci_coverage.py` keeps it **1:1 with the registry** so no benchmark silently gets zero coverage.
- **README rewrite**: replaced the "40+" framing with the honest breakdown (43 registered / ~25 bridge-wired-real / explicit CI lanes), documents what was deleted and the new lanes.

### Validation
- `pytest orchestrator/tests + tests` → **480 passed, 1 failed**. The one failure (`test_hermes_env_all_incomplete_zero_is_not_publishable`) is **pre-existing on `develop`** (hermes-env scoring, untouched by this PR, unrelated to #9475).
- `orchestrator list-benchmarks` → 51 adapters, **all benchmark dirs covered**.
- app-eval judge tests (9) + code-agent-coding tests green.
- New workflow YAML validated; comprehensive grep confirms **zero remaining references** to any deleted dir/module.

Closes #9475

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

topPRs.title: docs(8795): deep LifeOps scenario/benchmark/GEPA audit report + 53 verified findings
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Adds the research report requested on #8795 under `.github/issue-evidence/`.

Produced by a **10-subsystem parallel audit** (scenario-runner, PA scenarios, PA LLM consumers, health/screentime, scheduling spine, GEPA scoring, OptimizedPromptService, Python lifeops-bench, persona/fuzz lens, CI wiring) with **adversarial per-finding verification** — 53 of 56 candidate findings confirmed, 3 refuted. Plus hand-verified refinements on the highest-severity items.

## Headline findings (all file:line-anchored in the report)

- **CRITICAL — inverted/capped LIVE scorer.** The Python LIVE scorer caps a *correct* write agent at **0.3** (`scorer.py:1242-1244`): `expected_state_hash` replays an empty ground-truth on the seed (`runner.py:2855-2858`), so it equals the *unmutated* world and `state_component` is structurally unearnable for any world-mutating scenario → LIVE `pass@1` (≥0.99) is unreachable for every agent incl. PerfectAgent. The conformance oracle runs STATIC-only, which is why it shipped. *(Fix is a design decision — recommend extending the PerfectAgent/WrongAgent oracle to LIVE rather than a drive-by scoring rewrite.)*
- **HIGH — `execution.plannerText` is a dead field** (declared + read by `buildPlannerAssertionBlob`, assigned nowhere). `plannerIncludes*`/`plannerExcludes` are action-routing assertions mis-named as reasoning assertions.
- **HIGH — large larp surface in the PA corpus:** ~200 `personalityExpect` scenarios pass enforcing nothing (read only by `personality-bench`, never `runScenario`); 67% of `responseIncludesAny` turns are echo-satisfiable; 130 `executive.*` scenarios (77%) carry no load-bearing assertion; `plannerExcludes` lists exclude tokens that can never be emitted (`PAYMENT_EXECUTED` ×47).
- **HIGH — runtime bugs + dead targets:** `sleep-recap` pack can never fire (unknown `personal_baseline_sufficient` gate); bedtime/wake-up safety gates fall through to allow; Fitbit distance overstated ~2.5×; `meeting_prep`/`screentime_recap` are fully-plumbed but consumerless GEPA targets.
- **Safety/persona blind spots:** no scorer rewards a correct refusal/escalation; zero child/elderly/ESL/voice-transcription/adversarial coverage; no self-harm/crisis handling in health.

## Contents
- `8795-lifeops-audit-report.md` — full report (exec summary, system map, findings by severity, edge-case/fuzz/persona matrix, the `meeting_prep`/`screentime_recap` decision, a prioritized chunked work plan, and what's already solid).
- `8795-confirmed-findings.json` — the 53 confirmed findings (subsystem/kind/severity/evidence/fix) for traceability.

Evidence/docs only — no runtime code change. First code fix from this audit already merged: **#9540** (`health_checkin` trajectory tagging). Further fixes will land as small follow-up PRs.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

topPRs.title: test+feat: schedule_plan GEPA seed (#9299) + keyless mock-LLM monetization journey (#9477)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Two independent, fully-tested increments closing the actionable code follow-ups on their issues.

### `#9299` — `schedule_plan` LifeOps GEPA seed
The scheduling-negotiation planner (`SCHEDULE_PLAN_INSTRUCTIONS`) becomes a third lifeops GEPA seed task in `plugins/plugin-training/scripts/lifeops-gepa-seed.ts`, alongside `calendar_extract` and `inbox_triage`. The 11-row dataset covers every subaction (`start`/`propose`/`respond`/`finalize`/`cancel`/`list_active`/`list_proposals`), the wrong-tool + vague guards (`shouldAct=false`), and multilingual formal/informal phrasing (FR/ES) per the #9299 real-conversation requirement. The per-task persistence guard is extended so a degenerate optimized prompt that drops the subaction vocabulary can't replace the working baseline.

- **Test** (`lifeops-gepa-seed.test.ts`, 8/8, was 6): keys assertion updated; schedule_plan baseline/shape coverage; every-subaction-present check; multilingual check; malformed-persistence guard.

### `#9477` — keyless mock-LLM creator-monetization journey
Closes the two open code follow-ups on the (otherwise manual) #9477:
- An in-process OpenAI-compatible **mock LLM** (`mock-llm.ts`) + a `mockLlm` stack option that points the worker's `OPENAI_BASE_URL`/`OPENAI_API_KEY` at it (synced into `.dev.vars` via `providerOverrideKeys`). The new `monetized-mock-llm-journey.spec.ts` drives the **real** `POST /api/v1/messages` + `x-app-id` billing/markup/earnings seam against an `openai/<model>` id with **no paid key** — the gap the `CEREBRAS_API_KEY`-gated `creator-monetization-journey.spec.ts` left.
- Fixes the stale doc path `app/api/cron/container-billing/route.ts` → `packages/cloud-api/cron/container-billing/route.ts` in `survival-economics.md`.

- **Verified** (1 passed, 8.7s): end-user org debited `0.000016` (base + 100% markup), creator `app_earnings` `0 → 0.000008`, the mock served the inference. Only the LLM bytes are mocked; credits/markup/earnings are fully real.

## Verification
- `plugin-training` typecheck: turbo 30/30 green; `lifeops-gepa-seed.test.ts` 8/8
- `monetized-mock-llm-journey.spec.ts`: 1 passed (real debit + markup asserted)
- `cloud-e2e` tsc: only the pre-existing tsconfig `baseUrl` deprecation; no errors in touched files
- biome format clean

topPRs.title: feat(voice): sample-level AEC for live barge-in (#9455)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Closes the sample-level half of #9455 (split from #9147, which only did turn-level self-echo rejection).

## What
The live half-duplex voice path had no **sample-level** AEC: it could reject a whole turn that was the agent's own voice (`selfVoiceSimilarity` WeSpeaker cosine in `respond-gate.ts`), but it could not recover a **barge-in during playback** — the residual of the agent's own TTS still corrupted the user's transcript. This adds the per-sample layer.

New module `plugins/plugin-local-inference/src/services/voice/aec.ts`:
- A length-`L` FIR filter models the acoustic echo path (speaker→room→mic); per mic sample, `echo_hat = wᵀ·x` over the delay-aligned far-end window, output `e = near − echo_hat`, and **normalized-LMS** adaptation `w ← w + μ·e·x/(xᵀx+δ)`.
- A **double-talk detector** freezes adaptation (but keeps filtering) when the near-end clearly exceeds the echo estimate — so a genuine barge-in is preserved and the filter never diverges onto the user's voice (with a hangover hold).
- `setDelaySamples()` for the measured mic↔playback latency; `erle()` helper; pure, dependency-free, 16 kHz mono fp32, mirrors `vad.ts` / `self-voice-imprint.ts`. The far-end reference is already available at the `VoiceScheduler.onAudio` tap.

## Verification (Linux-CPU, deterministic, model-free — fully CI-able)
`bunx vitest run src/services/voice/__tests__/aec.test.ts` → **4/4 pass**:
- **31.68 dB ERLE** on a synthetic decaying-IR echo path after NLMS convergence (≥20 dB asserted).
- Double-talk: near-end talker preserved (corr **0.999**), DT detector fires, and a following echo-only segment stays cancelled (post-DT ERLE **74.75 dB**) — i.e. the filter froze and did not diverge.
- `erle()` edge cases + `process()` length/finite invariants.

## Scope
This is the linear AEC core (the bulk of the ERLE). The **AEC3-grade nonlinear residual suppressor + comfort noise**, and **live mic↔playback delay calibration + on-device ERLE/barge-in validation**, are the documented stage-2 (`needs-device`) follow-up the issue itself frames as "a further stage."

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

topPRs.title: feat(training): live GEPA optimization of LifeOps actions on gpt-oss-120b (#9299 Scope 5)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Part of #9299, **Scope 5** (GEPA-optimize the LifeOps actions for gpt-oss-120b). The seed harness was already wired (#9429/#9531) but no **live** before/after optimization had been run and no optimized artifacts produced — the marquee remaining DoD item. This PR runs it live and attaches measured results.

## Live before/after (gpt-oss-120b, gen=2 population=4)

| Task | Baseline | Optimized | Δ |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `calendar_extract` | 0.795 | 0.864 | **+0.068** |
| `inbox_triage` | 0.625 | 0.667 | **+0.042** |
| `schedule_plan` | _running; appended on completion_ | | |

Optimized prompts persisted to the standard optimized-prompt store (`OptimizedPromptService`) so they auto-load at boot. Artifacts + raw run logs are committed under `plugins/plugin-training/docs/audit/9299-gepa-live/` as human-verifiable evidence (these run logs ARE the real-LLM gpt-oss-120b trajectories for the optimization).

## Model/provider note (honest)

The issue says **gpt-oss-120b on Cerebras**; no `CEREBRAS_API_KEY` is present on this machine, so the run used the **identical `gpt-oss-120b` weights served by Together AI** (OpenAI-compatible) via the adapter's existing `CEREBRAS_BASE_URL` override. Same model, same seed harness, same persistence path — only the transport endpoint differs. `RESULTS.md` documents the exact re-run command for Cerebras.

## Code change

- `cerebras-eval-model.ts`: add `fetchChatWithRetry` — bounded retry (429 + 5xx + network) with exponential backoff. A single optimizer run fans out into dozens of provider calls; one transient `500` (common on serverless gpt-oss relays) previously aborted the whole generation, which is exactly why prior live runs couldn't complete. Also match `reasoning_effort` on the `<vendor>/gpt-oss-*` model form, not just the bare id. Existing seed/scoring unit tests pass (28/28).

## Scope-2 audit (bonus)

`DEPENDENCY_GRAPH.md` gains a domain-extraction status table: the modules flagged as "still embedded" are each in their correct home (calendar-gate + service-mixins are PA composition glue; email-classifier/entities/relationships are runtime shims). Flags `lifeops/bill-extraction.ts` as unconsumed finance scaffolding for the owner to wire into `plugin-finances` or remove.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

```
completedItems.body:
Surfaced while executing the manual monetized-app test of #9477.

## Manual test result (evidence)
I ran the deterministic monetized loop against the in-process mock stack:
```
bun --conditions=eliza-source --bun playwright test \
  monetiz
